description_long: This is a robust, freestanding hanger constructed primarily from thick, weathered wooden beams arranged in an X-frame structure for stability. The upper section features a horizontal rack made of several parallel metal rods, slightly rusted, suitable for hanging or drying items such as clothes, tools, or equipment. The wood displays a rough, aged texture with visible grain and some discoloration, suggesting outdoor use and exposure to the elements. The metal rods are securely fastened between the wooden supports, providing a sturdy surface for suspending objects. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, emphasizing function over form. This hanger would be well-suited for placement in urban outdoor environments such as parks, garages, or communal courtyards.
description: A sturdy, X-framed wooden hanger with a metal rod rack on top, designed for outdoor use and heavy-duty hanging tasks.
description_view_0: Front view showing the X-frame wooden supports and the full width of the metal rod rack.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the hanger and the parallel arrangement of the metal rods.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with clear visibility of the wooden frame and metal rack.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again emphasizing the X-frame and the alignment of the rods.
